name: integrations
context: Build Notion integrations — internal integrations and public OAuth apps
info: This skill covers building both types of Notion integrations: internal integrations (private to a single workspace, using a fixed token) and public integrations (distributed via OAuth 2.0, usable across any workspace). Internal integrations are the quickest way to get started — create one in the Notion integrations dashboard, share the target pages or databases with it, and use the provided token. Public integrations require OAuth configuration: redirect URI, client ID, client secret, and the authorization code exchange flow. Both integration types can request granular capabilities: read content, update content, insert content, read comments, create comments, and read user information.
body:
# Notion Integrations

Integrations are the gateway to the Notion API. Every API call requires an integration token. Notion supports two integration types: internal (private, single workspace) and public (OAuth, any workspace).

## Internal Setup

Internal integrations are the fastest path to API access:

1. **Create** — go to [notion.so/my-integrations](https://www.notion.so/my-integrations) and click "New integration"
2. **Configure** — name the integration, select the workspace, and choose capabilities (read/update/insert content, read/create comments)
3. **Copy token** — the integration token starts with `ntn_` (store it securely as `NOTION_TOKEN`)
4. **Share** — in Notion, open the target page or database, click "..." > "Connections" > add your integration

```javascript
const { Client } = require('@notionhq/client');
const notion = new Client({ auth: process.env.NOTION_TOKEN });

// Now you can call any API endpoint the integration has capabilities for
const pages = await notion.search({ query: 'Meeting Notes' });
```

Internal integrations only see content explicitly shared with them. This is a security feature — share only what the integration needs.

## OAuth Flow

Public integrations use OAuth 2.0 to access workspaces on behalf of users:

1. **Register** — create a public integration at [notion.so/my-integrations](https://www.notion.so/my-integrations) with a redirect URI
2. **Authorize** — redirect users to the Notion authorization URL:

```
https://api.notion.com/v1/oauth/authorize?
  client_id={NOTION_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID}&
  response_type=code&
  owner=user&
  redirect_uri={REDIRECT_URI}
```

3. **Exchange code** — POST to `/v1/oauth/token` with the authorization code:

```javascript
const response = await fetch('https://api.notion.com/v1/oauth/token', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: {
    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    Authorization: `Basic ${Buffer.from(
      `${process.env.NOTION_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID}:${process.env.NOTION_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET}`
    ).toString('base64')}`
  },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    grant_type: 'authorization_code',
    code: authorizationCode,
    redirect_uri: redirectUri
  })
});

const { access_token, bot_id, workspace_id } = await response.json();
```

4. **Use token** — the returned `access_token` works like an internal token but is scoped to the pages the user selected during authorization

## Permissions

Integrations request capabilities at creation time:

| Capability | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| Read content | Retrieve pages, databases, blocks |
| Update content | Update page properties, block content |
| Insert content | Create pages, append blocks |
| Read comments | List comments on pages and blocks |
| Create comments | Add comments to pages and blocks |
| Read user info | Access workspace member details |

Capabilities cannot be changed after creation for internal integrations. Public integrations can update capabilities, but users must re-authorize.

## Sharing

Integrations only access content explicitly shared with them. Share pages or databases via the Notion UI: open the item, click "..." > "Connections" > select the integration. Child pages and databases inherit the connection from their parent. For public integrations, users choose what to share during the OAuth authorization flow.

sections.title: Internal integration setup
sections.body: Create at notion.so/my-integrations. Select workspace, name, and capabilities. Copy the `ntn_` token and store as `NOTION_TOKEN`. Share target pages/databases with the integration via the Notion UI ("..." > Connections). Internal integrations are limited to a single workspace. Token does not expire but can be regenerated. See [[notion#authentication]] for request header format.

sections.title: OAuth 2.0 flow
sections.body: Register a public integration with a redirect URI. Redirect users to `https://api.notion.com/v1/oauth/authorize` with `client_id`, `response_type=code`, `owner=user`, and `redirect_uri`. After user approval, Notion redirects back with a `code` parameter. Exchange the code at `POST /v1/oauth/token` using Basic auth (client_id:client_secret base64-encoded). The response includes `access_token`, `bot_id`, `workspace_id`, and `workspace_name`. Tokens do not expire but users can revoke access. Store tokens securely per-workspace.

sections.title: Capabilities and permissions
sections.body: Integrations declare capabilities: read content, update content, insert content, read comments, create comments, read user info. Request only the capabilities your integration needs (principle of least privilege). Internal integration capabilities are fixed at creation. Public integration capability changes require user re-authorization. All API calls are scoped to shared content — even with read content capability, the integration cannot see pages not shared with it.

sections.title: Content sharing
sections.body: Integrations only access explicitly shared content. In the Notion UI, use "..." > "Connections" to add an integration to a page or database. Children inherit parent connections. For databases, sharing the database also grants access to all pages within it. For public OAuth integrations, users select which pages to share during the authorization flow. Use `notion.search()` to discover all content the integration can access. See [[notion/pages]] and [[notion/databases]] for working with shared content.

scripts.name: Setup Integration
scripts.description: Configure and test a Notion integration, including OAuth token exchange for public integrations

NOTION_TOKEN.description: Notion integration token (ntn_...) for internal integrations

NOTION_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID.description: OAuth client ID for public integrations

NOTION_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET.description: OAuth client secret for public integrations

type.description: Integration type: internal (fixed token) or public (OAuth)

botId.description: Bot user ID for the integration
